key_insight: SciPhyRL turns dynamic portfolio optimization into a data-driven Hamilton-Jacobi PDE problem, enabling one-shot offline learning of a cost-aware policy from historical trajectories.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical framework for institutional trading systems that need multi-period allocation decisions with explicit transaction costs, turnover control, and volatility management.
method: Extend the state with cumulative costs and optimize in continuous time.
Project the HJB equation onto observed paths and solve it with a PINN in one offline sweep.
Replace continuous trading rate control with discrete target holdings for short-horizon practicality.
evidence_signal: The approach is evaluated on a 14-asset ETF universe with an engineered oracle signal and reports strong out-of-sample Sharpe ratio gains over static and myopic baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the offline data and the engineered signal, and the PDE/PINN setup may be sensitive to model specification and training stability.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
